title: South of England: Standen, Capel, Petworth & Churt

main_route.excerpt: 9.40. we leave for the south of England.
We visit Standen, the Beale family home, a lovely Arts & Crafts house 1880.
Sonja lives in a big house at Capel ... We had a good meal & slept in the van on the drive.
we went to Mass with Sonja St. John's Horsham.
Off to Partridge Green where we are on a Farm *** with easy cycling onto the Downs Link cycle path.
We set off for Petworth. We shopped in the town for lunch tomorrow ... then looked at the house. Amazing paintings, wood carvings, kitchens.

main_route.normalized_name: Salmon's Bridge Farm, West Sussex, UK
main_route.excerpt: We tried to see a bit of Salmon's Bridge Farm, the Campins home that Simon visited when he was 10, then continued on to Jane in Churt.
continued on to Jane in Churt. We had supper & slept at Longwood House.
We had supper at the White Horse with Charles + Jenny + drove on home.

excursions.label: Downs Link cycle ride (Shoreham–Steyning), 10k each way

stops.excerpt: At Steyning we had a stupendous tea - rhubarb scones, rhubarb jam & then set off home, back to the van

[glued-in map: Route of the Downs Link ... places labelled: Shoreham, South Downs, Clay Vale, Greenwood Rdge, Southwater ... Cowfold ... North Downs, Guildford. Handwritten annotation on map: "we cycled 10k & 10k back"]

excursions.label: Tuesday: Ash Vale factory, the Lightbox (Gertrude Jekyll exhibition) & Kate Ruby's house

stops.excerpt: Simon drove us to the factory, Ash Vale & Jane & Eve caught the train to go to the Light box exhibition on Gertrude Jekyll 1843-1932.

stops.original_wording: the Light box exhibition on Gertrude Jekyll
stops.excerpt: Jane & Eve caught the train to go to the Light box exhibition on Gertrude Jekyll 1843-1932.

unresolved.issue: Place name illegible/uncertain. Tuesday after dropping Jane back at Churt the diary reads 'went on to Surs [Jew]' then (next page) 'to admire the new flat that Charles' landlord is preparing for him'. Likely the town where Charles lives, but the name cannot be read; possibly a misread of a Surrey/Sussex town. Cannot locate.
unresolved.excerpt: After lunch we drove Jane back to Churt & went on to Surs [Jew]

boundary_flags.issue: Top of page is the tail of the PREVIOUS trip (Sunday May 26: Annette's care, Simon's bike ride, failed visit to Richard Holroyd in Cambridge, then drove home). This trip starts at the heading 'Saturday June 15th' / '9.40. we leave for the south of England.'
Bottom of page closes this trip ('drove on home'); the page is flagged boundary_mixed and the next trip likely begins after the Gertrude Jekyll drawing.

summary: A four-day June 2013 trip from Birmingham to the south of England (Surrey/Sussex), visiting the National Trust Arts & Crafts house at Standen, basing with Sonja at Capel and on a farm near Partridge Green (with a Downs Link cycle ride to Steyning), then touring Petworth House, looking at the Campin family's Salmon's Bridge Farm, and staying with Jane at Longwood House in Churt before a final Tuesday of family visits around Ash Vale and the Lightbox in Woking, then driving home.
extraction_notes: The only sketch page (B_0213_R) is a glued-in PRINTED terrain cross-section titled 'Route of the Downs Link' (Shoreham→Guildford) annotated 'we cycled 10k & 10k back' — it documents the day cycle EXCURSION, not the trip's overnight order, so narrative order governs the main route here. Overnight sequencing is partly ambiguous: they slept in the van at Capel Saturday night; Sunday they were 'on a Farm ***' at Partridge Green (treated as overnight_base, but B_0214_L 'We reached Capel about 6 & spent the evening at Sonja's flat' suggests they may instead have returned to Capel for Sunday night). Petworth (Monday) is a day visit, not an overnight; the Monday overnight was Longwood House, Churt. Travellers listed conservatively as Simon and Eve (the diary's 'we'); Charles and Jenny appear only as people met locally. Salmon's Bridge Farm marked approximate (small named farm, briefly attempted, not toured). 'Surs [Jew]' is illegible and queued as unresolved. No sea/tunnel crossings — entirely within Great Britain.
